tools.name: convert
tools.description: Convert between different measurement units. Supports length, weight, temperature, area, volume, speed, time, and data units. Use this for any unit conversion between metric, imperial, and digital units. For listing available units or seeing categories, use list_units, categories, or help instead.

tools.name: list_units
tools.description: List all available units in a specific category. Use this to see which units are supported for conversion in a given category. For performing actual conversions, use convert instead.

category.description: The category to list units for (e.g., 'length', 'weight', 'temperature', 'area', 'volume', 'speed', 'time', 'data')

tools.name: categories
tools.description: Show all supported unit conversion categories. Use this to discover what types of conversions are available. For listing units within a specific category, use list_units instead.

tools.name: help
tools.description: Show detailed help and information for a specific unit category. Use this to learn about the units and conversion formulas available for a category. For listing all categories, use categories instead.

category.description: The category to show help for (e.g., 'length', 'weight', 'temperature', 'area', 'volume', 'speed', 'time', 'data')

messages.content: Hi there! I'm working on some writing that involves international data and perspectives, and I often need to convert different kinds of measurements. Could you tell me what types of unit conversions you can help with? I'm especially interested in things like distance, temperature, and maybe even population or area measurements that might come up when comparing different countries.
Great question! Let me show you what conversion categories are available.
{"status": "success", "tool": "categories", "categories": ["length", "weight", "temperature", "area", "volume", "speed", "time", "data"]}
messages.name: skill-tools-categories

messages.content:
I can help with **8 categories** of unit conversions:

| Category | What it includes |
|----------|------------------|
| **Length** | kilometers, miles, meters, feet, inches, yards, etc. |
| **Weight** | pounds, kilograms, grams, ounces, tons, etc. |
| **Temperature** | Fahrenheit, Celsius, Kelvin |
| **Area** | square miles, square kilometers, acres, hectares, square meters |
| **Volume** | gallons, liters, cubic meters, fluid ounces |
| **Speed** | mph, km/h, knots, m/s |
| **Time** | hours, minutes, seconds, days, weeks |
| **Data** | bytes, kilobytes, megabytes, gigabytes, terabytes |

For your international writing needs, you'll probably find **length** (for distances), **temperature** (weather/climate comparisons), and **area** (country sizes) most useful.

A couple of notes:
- **Population** isn't a unit conversion — it's just a raw number. You'd compare that directly (e.g., "Japan has ~125 million people").
- For comparing countries, **area** conversions are handy (square km ↔ square miles) and **length** for distances between cities.

Would you like me to show you the specific units available within any of these categories? Just let me know!
Actually, that would be helpful! I'm working on a piece comparing summer temperatures between the US and Europe. Could you convert 85 degrees Fahrenheit to Celsius for me?
{"status": "success", "tool": "convert", "input": {"value": 85.0, "from_unit": "fahrenheit", "to_unit": "celsius"}, "result": 29.444444444444443, "formatted": "85.0°F = 29.44°C"}
messages.name: skill-tools-convert

messages.content:
**85°F = 29.44°C**

That's a warm summer day! For reference:

- **85°F (29.4°C)** — Typical summer afternoon in places like Madrid, Rome, or Phoenix
- In Europe, many countries use Celsius, so you'd write: *"Temperatures reached nearly 30°C"*

If you need more conversions for your piece (maybe comparing average highs/lows, record temperatures, etc.), just let me know what values you'd like converted!

That was really helpful, thanks! While we're on the topic of temperature - you mentioned Kelvin as well. Could you show me what temperature units are available overall? I want to make sure I know all my options for when I'm writing about different climate contexts.
{"status": "success", "tool": "list_units", "category": "temperature", "units": ["celsius", "fahrenheit", "kelvin", "rankine"]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_units

messages.content:
Here are the **4 temperature units** available:

| Unit | Common Use |
|------|------------|
| **Celsius (°C)** | Most countries worldwide, scientific contexts |
| **Fahrenheit (°F)** | United States, a few Caribbean territories |
| **Kelvin (K)** | Scientific/research applications, thermodynamics |
| **Rankine (°R)** | Engineering contexts (especially in the US) |

For your climate writing:
- **Celsius** is your go-to for most international contexts (Europe, Canada, Australia, etc.)
- **Fahrenheit** for US-based comparisons
- **Kelvin** is mainly for scientific writing — you probably won't need it for general climate pieces

Would you like to convert between any of these? Or should I show you the units for another category like **area** (useful for comparing country sizes)?

That would be great, actually! Since I'm writing about international comparisons, area units might be really useful for talking about country sizes. Could you show me what units are available in the area category?

{"status": "success", "tool": "list_units", "category": "area", "units": ["square_meters", "square_kilometers", "square_centimeters", "square_millimeters", "hectares", "acres", "square_feet", "square_inches", "square_yards", "square_miles"]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_units

messages.content:
Here are the **10 area units** available:

| Unit | Best For |
|------|----------|
| **Square kilometers (km²)** | Countries, large regions — the international standard |
| **Square miles (mi²)** | US/UK contexts, comparing US states |
| **Hectares (ha)** | Agricultural land, parks, large properties |
| **Acres** | US/UK real estate, farmland |
| **Square meters (m²)** | Buildings, rooms, small spaces |
| **Square feet (ft²)** | US real estate, indoor spaces |
| **Square yards (yd²)** | Fabric, landscaping (US) |
| **Square centimeters (cm²)** | Small surfaces, scientific |
| **Square millimeters (mm²)** | Tiny precision areas |

For your international writing:
- **Square kilometers** is the global standard for country/region sizes (e.g., *"France covers 644,000 km²"*)
- **Square miles** is common in US contexts (e.g., *"Texas is ~268,000 mi²"*)
- **Hectares** is handy for talking about farmland or forests

Would you like me to convert some country sizes for you? I can do things like square miles ↔ square kilometers!

That was really helpful! Now that I've seen temperature and area units, I realize length might also come in handy for my writing - especially when comparing distances between cities in different countries. Could you show me what units are available in the length category?

{"status": "success", "tool": "list_units", "category": "length", "units": ["meter", "kilometer", "centimeter", "millimeter", "micrometer", "nanometer", "mile", "yard", "foot", "inch", "nautical_mile", "light_year"]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_units

messages.content:
Here are the **12 length units** available:

| Unit | Best For |
|------|----------|
| **Kilometers (km)** | International road distances, country-to-country |
| **Miles (mi)** | US/UK road distances, running races |
| **Meters (m)** | General purpose, sports tracks |
| **Yards (yd)** | American football, fabric (US) |
| **Feet (ft)** | US aviation, building heights |
| **Inches (in)** | Small measurements, US contexts |
| **Nautical miles (nmi)** | Maritime and aviation navigation |
| **Centimeters (cm)** | Small objects, weather forecasts (snow) |
| **Millimeters (mm)** | Rainfall, precision measurements |
| **Micrometers (μm)** | Scientific/technical |
| **Nanometers (nm)** | Scientific (atomic scale) |
| **Light-years (ly)** | Astronomy |

For your writing:
- **Kilometers** — standard for most international contexts (e.g., *"Paris is 1,050 km from Berlin"*)
- **Miles** — US and UK road signs, though UK uses km too now
- **Nautical miles** — useful if you're writing about flights or shipping routes

Would you like to convert some distances? I can do things like miles ↔ kilometers!

That gives me a great foundation! I've got temperature, area, and length covered now. One more that might come in handy for my writing - could you show me what weight units are available? I might need to compare things like vehicle weights or shipping capacities between countries.
{"status": "success", "tool": "list_units", "category": "weight", "units": ["kilogram", "gram", "milligram", "microgram", "metric_ton", "pound", "ounce", "stone", "short_ton", "long_ton", "carat"]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_units

messages.content:
Here are the **12 weight units** available:

| Unit | Best For |
|------|----------|
| **Kilograms (kg)** | International standard, everyday weights |
| **Metric tons (t)** | Shipping, freight, large quantities |
| **Grams (g)** | Food nutrition, small items |
| **Milligrams (mg)** | Medicine, scientific |
| **Micrograms (μg)** | Scientific/technical |
| **Carats (ct)** | Gemstones, jewelry |
| **Pounds (lb)** | US everyday use, body weight |
| **Ounces (oz)** | US food, small packages |
| **Stone (st)** | Body weight (UK/Ireland) |
| **Short tons (ton)** | US shipping, industry |
| **Long tons (tonne)** | UK shipping, naval |

For your writing:
- **Kilograms** — the global standard (e.g., *"The average European car weighs ~1,500 kg"*)
- **Metric tons** — shipping containers, cargo (e.g., *"The port handles 2 million tons of cargo annually"*)
- **Pounds** — US vehicle weights, payload ratings
- **Short tons vs Long tons** — US uses short tons (2,000 lbs), UK uses long tons (2,240 lbs), metric ton is 1,000 kg

Would you like to convert some weights? I can do things like pounds ↔ kilograms or tons ↔ metric tons!

This has been really helpful! I've now got a good overview of the main categories I'd need for my international writing - temperature, area, length, and weight. I think I have everything I need to proceed with my piece. Thanks so much for walking me through all these options!

[TASK_END]

You're very welcome! I'm glad I could help you get set up for your international writing.

Here's a quick recap of what we covered:

| Category | Units Available |
|----------|-----------------|
| **Temperature** | Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin, Rankine |
| **Area** | Square km, square miles, hectares, acres, and more |
| **Length** | Kilometers, miles, meters, feet, nautical miles, etc. |
| **Weight** | Kilograms, metric tons, pounds, ounces, and more |

Good luck with your piece! If you need any conversions as you write — whether it's comparing country sizes, city-to-city distances, climate data, or anything else — just let me know. Happy writing! 🌍✍️

This has been really helpful! I've now got a good overview of the main categories I'd need for my international writing - temperature, area, length, and weight. I think I have everything I need to proceed with my piece. Thanks so much for walking me through all these options!

[TASK_END]

You're very welcome! I'm glad I could help you get set up for your international writing project. 

You now have all the tools you need:
- **Temperature** — Fahrenheit ↔ Celsius for climate comparisons
- **Area** — square kilometers ↔ square miles for country sizes  
- **Length** — miles ↔ kilometers for distances between cities
- **Weight** — pounds ↔ kilograms for vehicles and cargo

Feel free to come back anytime if you need conversions as you write. Good luck with your piece! 📝🌎

This has been really helpful! I've now got a good overview of the main categories I'd need for my international writing - temperature, area, length, and weight. I think I have everything I need to proceed with my piece. Thanks so much for walking me through all these options!

[TASK_END]
You're welcome! Best of luck with your writing — enjoy your international comparisons! 🚀📊

Oh, wait - I just realized I haven't asked about volume units yet! Those could be useful for comparing things like water usage or beverage consumption between countries. Could you show me what's available in the volume category?

{"status": "success", "tool": "list_units", "category": "volume", "units": ["liter", "milliliter", "gallon_us", "gallon_uk", "quart", "pint", "cup", "fluid_ounce", "cubic_meter", "cubic_centimeter", "cubic_inch", "cubic_foot", "tablespoon", "teaspoon"]}
messages.name: skill-tools-list_units

messages.content:
Here are the **14 volume units** available:

| Unit | Best For |
|------|----------|
| **Liters (L)** | International standard, beverages, fuel |
| **Milliliters (mL)** | Small liquid quantities, medicine |
| **Cubic meters (m³)** | Large-scale (swimming pools, shipping containers) |
| **Gallons (US)** | US fuel, milk, beverages |
| **Gallons (UK)** | UK fuel, beer barrels |
| **Quarts (qt)** | US cooking, beverages |
| **Pints (pt)** | US/UK beer, dairy |
| **Cups** | US cooking recipes |
| **Fluid ounces (fl oz)** | US beverages, cocktails |
| **Cubic centimeters (cm³)** | Engine displacement, medical |
| **Cubic inches (in³)** | US engine sizes |
| **Cubic feet (ft³)** | US HVAC, shipping |
| **Tablespoons (tbsp)** | US cooking |
| **Teaspoons (tsp)** | US cooking |

For your writing:
- **Liters** — global standard for beverages (e.g., *"Germany consumes ~140 liters of beer per person annually"*)
- **Gallons (US)** — US fuel efficiency, milk
- **Gallons (UK)** — UK fuel (though liters are now common)
- **Cubic meters** — water usage, reservoir capacities

Would you like to convert some volumes? Things like gallons ↔ liters could be handy for comparing beverage consumption!

That covers everything I was curious about! I've now seen all the main categories - temperature, area, length, weight, and volume - so I really do have everything I need for my writing. Thanks so much for walking me through all these options!

[TASK_END]

You're very welcome! You now have the full picture:

- ✅ Temperature
- ✅ Area
- ✅ Length
- ✅ Weight
- ✅ Volume

You're all set for your international writing project. Good luck, and feel free to return if you need any conversions along the way! 🌍✍️
